text.title: Cross-Tool Migration Basics Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the tool-migration-basics methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the final rule defines when to skip this methodology entirely.

rule.statement: Source export MUST exist (CSV/JSON) before any field-mapping work begins.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Post-migration row count MUST match source count (±0); any drift blocks cutover.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Any lossy mapping (field dropped, type coerced) MUST be documented in the mapping table with stakeholder sign-off.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Migration MUST have a documented rollback procedure (source kept read-only ≥30 days).
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Migration MUST be run end-to-end on a staging board with ≥10 issues before production.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Skip when >3 boards or >500 issues — use tool-migration-process.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.
